title: Expand storage and hauling beyond the M1 fixture
summary: Extend storage supply/demand and hauling jobs beyond the single M1 fixture while preserving integer quantity ownership and bounded indexed work selection.
acceptance: Multiple source stacks storage destinations and demand slots update indexes from owner-state changes only
Hauling jobs reserve source amount destination capacity and interaction spots before pickup
Pickup delivery cancellation fail and interruption paths conserve integer quantities exactly once
Storage supply/demand dirty queues drain without sustained backlog growth under the M2 fixture
No broad economy crafting balance or content catalog expansion is introduced

history.details: Created by WM-0032 M2 planning DAG; remains proposed until dependencies are done.

objective: Generalize M1 hauling enough for the M2 vertical slice without broad economy work.
forbiddenPaths: packages/sim-worker/** unless blocked for parity planning first
balance tables or production content catalogs
benchmarkImpact: Extends logistics artifacts with multiple source/destination conservation, dirty backlog, reservation and candidate cap metrics.
reviewRouting: Owner completes to reviewer; qa-performance confirms benchmark artifact reproducibility when baseline data changes.
rollbackModel: Revert branch and M2 logistics artifacts while preserving M1 hauling/building scenario behavior.
ownerGate: Document quantity owner invariants and dirty queue caps
Run every required check before taskctl complete

sparkEligibility.reason: Storage and hauling touch state ownership, reservations, quantities and deterministic logistics.
